cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020, and 176 days have passed since then. The question is asking for the date tomorrow in MM/DD/YYYY format, and we have to pick from the options given. Alright, first I need to figure out what date is 176 days after March 20, 2020, and then add one more day to get the "tomorrow" date. Let me start by breaking down the days. March 20, 2020. Let me count the days remaining in March. March has 31 days, so from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days (since we include March 20 as the day she quit, but the next day would be March 21). Wait, actually, if she quit on March 20, then the next day after that is March 21. But when calculating 176 days from March 20, we need to count March 20 as day 0 or day 1? Wait, the problem says 176 days have passed since then. So if she quit on March 20, then day 1 would be March 21, day 2 March 22, and so on. So the date 176 days after March 20 would be the end of day 176. Then tomorrow would be that date plus one day. Alternatively, maybe I should use a date calculator approach. Let me first compute the date that is 176 days after March 20, 2020. Then add one day to get tomorrow's date. But since I don't have a calendar here, I need to do this manually. Let's start by adding months and days step by step. March has 31 days. So from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days (March 20 to March 31 inclusive is 12 days, but since March 20 is the starting point, the days after that would be 11 days left in March). Wait, March 20 to March 21 is 1 day, March 20 to March 31 is 11 days. So March contributes 11 days. So subtract 11 days from 176, we have 176-11=165 days left. Next is April. April has 30 days. So adding 30 days to the 11 days in March, we have 11+30=41 days. Then remaining days would be 176-41=135 days left. Then May has 31 days. So adding May's 31 days: 41+31=72 days. Remaining days: 176-72=104 days left. June has 30 days. Adding June's 30 days: 72+30=102 days. Remaining days: 176-102=74 days left. July has 31 days. Adding July's 31 days: 102+31=133 days. Remaining days: 176-133=43 days left. August has 31 days. Adding August's 31 days: 133+31=164 days. Remaining days: 176-164=12 days left. So up to August 31, 2020, we have 164 days. So the remaining 12 days would be in September. So adding 12 days to September 1, 2020, gives us September 12, 2020. Wait, but wait, let me check again. Wait, March 20 to March 31: 11 days April 1 to April 30: 30 days (total so far: 11+30=41) May 1 to May 31: 31 days (total so far: 41+31=72) June 1 to June 30: 30 days (total so far: 72+30=102) July 1 to July 31: 31 days (total so far: 102+31=133) August 1 to August 31: 31 days (total so far: 133+31=164) Now we have 176-164=12 days left in September. So adding 12 days to September 1 is September 12. So the date 176 days after March 20, 2020 is September 12, 2020. Then tomorrow would be September 13, 2020. Looking at the options, option A is 09/13/2020, which would be tomorrow's date if today
